model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.name: agg_hours_per_project
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.description: Total hours per project from the clean fact mart. project_id is a DB-enforced primary key (the aggregation grain); total_hours is NOT NULL by construction.
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.raw_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project
-- Total hours per project from the clean fact mart. Reads ONLY from
-- fct_timesheets (single source of accepted, enriched facts); no re-joins.
-- project_id uniqueness (the aggregation grain) is asserted here.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    count(*)::integer                     as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)            as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer  as distinct_employees
from {{ ref('fct_timesheets') }}
group by project_id, project_name
order by total_hours desc
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.compiled_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project
-- Total hours per project from the clean fact mart. Reads ONLY from
-- fct_timesheets (single source of accepted, enriched facts); no re-joins.
-- project_id uniqueness (the aggregation grain) is asserted here.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    count(*)::integer                     as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)            as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer  as distinct_employees
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."fct_timesheets"
group by project_id, project_name
order by total_hours desc

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Project PK (DB-enforced). One row per project that has timesheets (21 rows).

columns.name: project_name
columns.description: Project name (denormalized from dim_projects).

columns.name: timesheet_count
columns.description: Number of accepted timesheet entries for this project.

columns.name: total_hours
columns.description: Total logged hours for this project (sum of fct_timesheets.hours). NOT NULL by construction.

columns.name: distinct_employees
columns.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours on this project.

model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.name: agg_hours_per_project_monthly
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.description: Monthly hours per project (project x month). Adds the time dimension to the per-project rollup, revealing each project's burn-down/tempo over time. One row per (project_id, month). Reads fct_timesheets.
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.raw_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project_monthly
-- Monthly hours per project (project x month). Adds the TIME dimension to the
-- per-project rollup, revealing each project's burn-down / tempo over time --
-- a flat annual total hides this. Derives the month from the fact date directly
-- (no dim_date needed). One row per (project_id, month). Reads only fct_timesheets.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date) as month,
    count(*)::integer                    as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)           as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer as distinct_employees
from {{ ref('fct_timesheets') }}
group by project_id, project_name, cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date)
order by project_id, month
model.dbt_pipeline.agg_hours_per_project.compiled_sql:
-- agg_hours_per_project_monthly
-- Monthly hours per project (project x month). Adds the TIME dimension to the
-- per-project rollup, revealing each project's burn-down / tempo over time --
-- a flat annual total hides this. Derives the month from the fact date directly
-- (no dim_date needed). One row per (project_id, month). Reads only fct_timesheets.

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date) as month,
    count(*)::integer                    as timesheet_count,
    sum(hours)::decimal(12, 2)           as total_hours,
    count(distinct employee_id)::integer as distinct_employees
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."fct_timesheets"
group by project_id, project_name, cast(date_trunc('month', date) as date)
order by project_id, month

model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.name: dim_employees
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.description: Clean employee dimension: one row per employee_id from stg_employees (the dedup survivors). Soft-issue rows (null name E013, null role E021) are retained since the id is valid. Contracted; employee_id is a DB-enforced primary key and the FK target for fct_timesheets.
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.raw_sql:
-- dim_employees
-- Clean employee dimension: one row per employee_id from stg_employees (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Soft-issue rows (null name/role) are retained
-- since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the PK test. Reads only
-- stg_employees (single atomic source).

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from {{ ref('stg_employees') }}
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_employees.compiled_sql:

-- Clean employee dimension: one row per employee_id from stg_employees (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Soft-issue rows (null name/role) are retained
-- since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the PK test. Reads only
-- stg_employees (single atomic source).

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_employees"

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Employee PK (DB-enforced primary key). One row per employee_id (40 rows).

columns.description: Employee name; nullable (NULL for E013 — review row kept).

model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.name: dim_projects
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.description: Clean project dimension: one row per project_id from stg_projects (the dedup survivors). Soft-issue rows (null name P013, null/invalid budget P008/P032/P019) are retained since the id is valid. Negative budget is nullified here. Contracted; project_id is a DB-enforced primary key and the FK target for fct_timesheets.
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.raw_sql:
-- dim_projects
-- Clean project dimension: one row per project_id from stg_projects (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Negative budget is nullified here (the
-- consumer-facing dim). Soft-issue rows (null project_name, null/invalid
-- budget) are retained since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the
-- PK test. Reads only stg_projects (single atomic source).

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    case when budget < 0 then null else budget end as budget
from {{ ref('stg_projects') }}
model.dbt_pipeline.dim_projects.compiled_sql:
-- dim_projects
-- Clean project dimension: one row per project_id from stg_projects (the
-- dedup survivors, non-null id). Negative budget is nullified here (the
-- consumer-facing dim). Soft-issue rows (null project_name, null/invalid
-- budget) are retained since the id is valid. Contracted FK target with the
-- PK test. Reads only stg_projects (single atomic source).

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    case when budget < 0 then null else budget end as budget
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_projects"

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Project PK (DB-enforced primary key). One row per project_id (35 rows).

columns.name: project_name
columns.description: Project name; nullable (NULL for P013 — review row kept).

columns.name: budget
columns.description: Project budget as decimal(12,2); NULL for P008 (non-numeric source), P019 (negative, nullified), P032 (null source). Review rows kept with NULL budget.

model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.name: fct_timesheets
model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.description: Clean timesheets fact mart: the accepted fact set from int_timesheets_validated left-joined to dim_employees and dim_projects. Pure join, no filters — int already removed dangling FKs and single-source rejects, so the grain is clean (one row per timesheet_id). timesheet_id is a DB-enforced primary key; employee_id / project_id FK integrity is enforced upstream by int_timesheets_validated (prevention) and asserted here by relationships tests (detection).
model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.raw_sql:
-- fct_timesheets
-- The clean timesheets fact mart: the accepted fact set from
-- int_timesheets_validated joined to dim_employees / dim_projects. Pure join,
-- no filters -- int already removed dangling FKs and single-source rejects, so
-- the grain is clean (one row per timesheet_id). PK (timesheet_id) and FK
-- (employee_id -> dim_employees, project_id -> dim_projects) tests run directly
-- on the columns here with no `where` filtering.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    emp.name as employee_name,
    emp.role as employee_role,
    ts.project_id,
    prj.project_name,
    prj.budget as project_budget,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from {{ ref('int_timesheets_validated') }} ts
left join {{ ref('dim_employees') }} emp on emp.employee_id = ts.employee_id
left join {{ ref('dim_projects') }}  prj on prj.project_id  = ts.project_id
model.dbt_pipeline.fct_timesheets.compiled_sql:
-- fct_timesheets
-- The clean timesheets fact mart: the accepted fact set from
-- int_timesheets_validated joined to dim_employees / dim_projects. Pure join,
-- no filters -- int already removed dangling FKs and single-source rejects, so
-- the grain is clean (one row per timesheet_id). PK (timesheet_id) and FK
-- (employee_id -> dim_employees, project_id -> dim_projects) tests run directly
-- on the columns here with no `where` filtering.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    emp.name as employee_name,
    emp.role as employee_role,
    ts.project_id,
    prj.project_name,
    prj.budget as project_budget,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_intermediate"."int_timesheets_validated" ts
left join "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."dim_employees" emp on emp.employee_id = ts.employee_id
left join "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."dim_projects"  prj on prj.project_id  = ts.project_id

model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.name: int_timesheets_validated
model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.description: The accepted fact set: stg_timesheets semi-joined to the clean stg_employees / stg_projects (IN subqueries, no fan-out) to filter dangling FKs. Feeds fct_timesheets. The only intermediate (connects multiple atomic sources).
model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.raw_sql:
-- int_timesheets_validated
-- The cross-source fact-cleaning intermediate: connects stg_timesheets to the
-- clean stg_employees / stg_projects and filters dangling FKs -> the accepted
-- fact set. This is the legitimate intermediate role (joins/references multiple
-- atomic staging models). Uses semi-joins (IN) against the clean dim-source
-- keys to avoid fan-out. No flags, no status column -- cleaning is a where
-- filter, not a flag. Dangling rows present in stg_timesheets (E099/E050/P999)
-- are dropped here.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    ts.project_id,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from {{ ref('stg_timesheets') }} ts
where ts.employee_id in (select employee_id from {{ ref('stg_employees') }} where employee_id is not null)
  and ts.project_id  in (select project_id  from {{ ref('stg_projects') }}  where project_id is not null)
model.dbt_pipeline.int_timesheets_valida.compiled_sql:
-- int_timesheets_validated
-- The cross-source fact-cleaning intermediate: connects stg_timesheets to the
-- clean stg_employees / stg_projects and filters dangling FKs -> the accepted
-- fact set. This is the legitimate intermediate role (joins/references multiple
-- atomic staging models). Uses semi-joins (IN) against the clean dim-source
-- keys to avoid fan-out. No flags, no status column -- cleaning is a where
-- filter, not a flag. Dangling rows present in stg_timesheets (E099/E050/P999)
-- are dropped here.

select
    ts.timesheet_id,
    ts.employee_id,
    ts.project_id,
    ts.date,
    ts.hours
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_timesheets" ts
where ts.employee_id in (select employee_id from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_employees" where employee_id is not null)
  and ts.project_id  in (select project_id  from "dbt_pipeline"."main_staging"."stg_projects"  where project_id is not null)

model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.name: rpt_project_economics
model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.description: Per-project economics: budget + logged hours + derived budget_per_hour (budget / total_hours). LEFT-joins from dim_projects so ALL projects appear (budgeted-but-not-started show 0 hours). ASSUMPTION: budget_per_hour is meaningful only if budget is a labor-cost budget; otherwise indicative only.
model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.raw_sql:
-- rpt_project_economics
-- Per-project economics: combines the project budget (dim_projects) with logged
-- hours (the per-project rollup) to derive cost-per-hour. LEFT-joins from
-- dim_projects, so it includes ALL projects -- budgeted-but-not-started projects
-- appear with 0 hours, highlighting work that is funded but not yet staffed.
--
-- ASSUMPTION: budget_per_hour = budget / total_hours is meaningful only if
-- `budget` is a labor-cost budget comparable to hours. If `budget` is a total
-- project budget (incl. non-labor costs), treat budget_per_hour as indicative
-- only. budget_per_hour is NULL for projects with no hours or a NULL budget.

select
    p.project_id,
    p.project_name,
    p.budget,
    coalesce(t.total_hours, 0)::decimal(12, 2)       as total_hours,
    coalesce(t.distinct_employees, 0)::integer       as distinct_employees,
    case
        when coalesce(t.total_hours, 0) = 0 then null
        else p.budget / t.total_hours
    end::decimal(18, 4)                              as budget_per_hour
from {{ ref('dim_projects') }} p
left join {{ ref('agg_hours_per_project') }} t on t.project_id = p.project_id
order by p.project_id
model.dbt_pipeline.rpt_project_economics.compiled_sql:
-- rpt_project_economics
-- Per-project economics: combines the project budget (dim_projects) with logged
-- hours (the per-project rollup) to derive cost-per-hour. LEFT-joins from
-- dim_projects, so it includes ALL projects -- budgeted-but-not-started projects
-- appear with 0 hours, highlighting work that is funded but not yet staffed.
--
-- ASSUMPTION: budget_per_hour = budget / total_hours is meaningful only if
-- `budget` is a labor-cost budget comparable to hours. If `budget` is a total
-- project budget (incl. non-labor costs), treat budget_per_hour as indicative
-- only. budget_per_hour is NULL for projects with no hours or a NULL budget.

select
    p.project_id,
    p.project_name,
    p.budget,
    coalesce(t.total_hours, 0)::decimal(12, 2)       as total_hours,
    coalesce(t.distinct_employees, 0)::integer       as distinct_employees,
    case
        when coalesce(t.total_hours, 0) = 0 then null
        else p.budget / t.total_hours
    end::decimal(18, 4)                              as budget_per_hour
from "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."dim_projects" p
left join "dbt_pipeline"."main_marts"."agg_hours_per_project" t on t.project_id = p.project_id
order by p.project_id

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Project PK (DB-enforced). One row per project (35 rows, including projects with no timesheets).

columns.name: project_name
columns.description: Project name from dim_projects.

columns.name: total_hours
columns.description: Total logged hours for this project (0 for budgeted-but-not-started projects).

columns.name: distinct_employees
columns.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours (0 for projects with no timesheets).

columns.name: budget_per_hour
columns.description: Derived: budget / total_hours. NULL when total_hours = 0 or budget is NULL. Assumes budget is a labor-cost budget (see model description).

model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.name: stg_employees
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.description: Typed, deduped projection of raw.employees: one row per non-null employee_id (dup_rank = 1 survivor). Soft-issue rows (null name/role) are KEPT since the id is valid.
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.raw_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        employee_id,
        name,
        role,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id) as dup_rank
    from {{ source('raw', 'employees') }}
)

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and employee_id is not null
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_employees.compiled_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        employee_id,
        name,
        role,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id) as dup_rank
    from "dbt_pipeline"."main_raw"."employees"
)

select
    employee_id,
    name,
    role
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and employee_id is not null

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Clean employee identifier (non-null; exact duplicates removed).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Employee name (NULL where empty -- E013).

columns.name: role
columns.description: Employee role (NULL where empty -- E021).

model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.name: stg_projects
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.description: Typed, deduped projection of raw.projects: one row per non-null project_id (dup_rank = 1 survivor). try_cast budget to decimal (non-numeric -> NULL). Soft-issue rows (null name, null/invalid/negative budget) are KEPT; negative budget is nullified in dim_projects.
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.raw_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        project_id,
        project_name,
        try_cast(budget as decimal(12, 2)) as budget,
        row_number() over (partition by project_id) as dup_rank
    from {{ source('raw', 'projects') }}
)

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    budget
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and project_id is not null
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_projects.compiled_sql:
with ranked as (
    select
        project_id,
        project_name,
        try_cast(budget as decimal(12, 2)) as budget,
        row_number() over (partition by project_id) as dup_rank
    from "dbt_pipeline"."main_raw"."projects"
)

select
    project_id,
    project_name,
    budget
from ranked
where dup_rank = 1
  and project_id is not null

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Clean project identifier (non-null; exact duplicates removed).

columns.name: project_name
columns.description: Project name (NULL where the source field was empty -- P013).

columns.name: budget
columns.description: Budget as decimal(12,2); non-numeric source values (e.g. '55000-60000' for P008) -> NULL. Negative (P019) preserved here, nullified in dim_projects.

model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.name: stg_timesheets
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.description: Typed, deduped projection of raw.timesheets with single-source hard rejects removed (missing employee_id, unparseable date, non-numeric/out-of-range hours). Cross-source dangling FKs (E099/E050/P999) are KEPT here and removed in int_timesheets_validated. Adds a deterministic surrogate PK timesheet_id.
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.raw_sql:
with src as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        date,
        hours,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id, project_id, date, hours) as dup_rank
    from {{ source('raw', 'timesheets') }}
),
typed as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        coalesce(
            try_cast(date as date),
            try_strptime(date, '%d/%m/%Y')::date,
            try_strptime(date, '%d-%b-%y')::date
        ) as date,
        try_cast(hours as decimal(5, 2)) as hours,
        dup_rank
    from src
)

select
    md5(
        coalesce(employee_id, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(project_id, '')  || '|' ||
        coalesce(date::varchar, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(hours::varchar, '')
    ) as timesheet_id,
    employee_id,
    project_id,
    date,
    hours
from typed
where dup_rank = 1                                      -- drop exact duplicates
  and employee_id is not null                            -- drop missing employee_id
  and date is not null                                    -- drop unparseable date
  and hours is not null                                   -- drop non-numeric hours
  and hours > 0 and hours <= 24                           -- drop out-of-range hours
model.dbt_pipeline.stg_timesheets.compiled_sql:
with src as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        date,
        hours,
        row_number() over (partition by employee_id, project_id, date, hours) as dup_rank
    from "dbt_pipeline"."main_raw"."timesheets"
),
typed as (
    select
        employee_id,
        project_id,
        coalesce(
            try_cast(date as date),
            try_strptime(date, '%d/%m/%Y')::date,
            try_strptime(date, '%d-%b-%y')::date
        ) as date,
        try_cast(hours as decimal(5, 2)) as hours,
        dup_rank
    from src
)

select
    md5(
        coalesce(employee_id, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(project_id, '')  || '|' ||
        coalesce(date::varchar, '') || '|' ||
        coalesce(hours::varchar, '')
    ) as timesheet_id,
    employee_id,
    project_id,
    date,
    hours
from typed
where dup_rank = 1                                      -- drop exact duplicates
  and employee_id is not null                            -- drop missing employee_id
  and date is not null                                    -- drop unparseable date
  and hours is not null                                   -- drop non-numeric hours
  and hours > 0 and hours <= 24                           -- drop out-of-range hours

columns.name: timesheet_id
columns.description: Deterministic surrogate PK = md5(employee_id|project_id|date|hours).

columns.name: employee_id

columns.name: project_id

columns.description: Raw employee role (VARCHAR). NULL for E021 — detected by not_null (warn). 8 valid roles validated by accepted_values source test.

source.dbt_pipeline.raw.projects.name: projects

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Raw project identifier (VARCHAR). Has 3 exact duplicates (P001/P007/P026) and 1 null row — detected by unique + not_null source tests.

insights.generated_description: Unique identifier for each record in projects.

columns.name: project_name
columns.description: Raw project name (VARCHAR). NULL for P013 — detected by not_null (warn) source test.

columns.name: budget

source.dbt_pipeline.raw.timesheets.name: timesheets
source.dbt_pipeline.raw.timesheets.description: Raw timesheets CSV (employee_id, project_id, date, hours) — 343 rows, all VARCHAR.

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Raw employee FK (VARCHAR). 1 null row, 2 dangling (E099/E050) — detected by not_null + relationships source tests.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: Raw project FK (VARCHAR). 2 dangling (P999) — detected by relationships source test. No nulls.

columns.name: date

search_index.name: agg_hours_per_project
search_index.description: Total hours per project from the clean fact mart. project_id is a DB-enforced primary key (the aggregation grain); total_hours is NOT NULL by construction.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project PK (DB-enforced). One row per project that has timesheets (21 rows).

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Project name (denormalized from dim_projects).

search_index.name: timesheet_count
search_index.description: Number of accepted timesheet entries for this project.

search_index.name: total_hours
search_index.description: Total logged hours for this project (sum of fct_timesheets.hours). NOT NULL by construction.

search_index.name: distinct_employees
search_index.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours on this project.

search_index.name: agg_hours_per_project_monthly
search_index.description: Monthly hours per project (project x month). Adds the time dimension to the per-project rollup, revealing each project's burn-down/tempo over time. One row per (project_id, month). Reads fct_timesheets.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project identifier; part of the composite PK (project_id, month).

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Project name (denormalized from the fact).

search_index.name: month
search_index.description: First day of the month (date_trunc('month', date)); part of the composite PK. One row per project-month with timesheets.

search_index.name: timesheet_count
search_index.description: Number of accepted timesheet entries for this project in this month.

search_index.name: total_hours
search_index.description: Total logged hours for this project in this month.

search_index.name: distinct_employees
search_index.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours on this project in this month.

search_index.name: dim_employees
search_index.description: Clean employee dimension: one row per employee_id from stg_employees (the dedup survivors). Soft-issue rows (null name E013, null role E021) are retained since the id is valid. Contracted; employee_id is a DB-enforced primary key and the FK target for fct_timesheets.

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Employee PK (DB-enforced primary key). One row per employee_id (40 rows).

search_index.name: name
search_index.description: Employee name; nullable (NULL for E013 — review row kept).

search_index.name: role
search_index.description: Employee role; nullable (NULL for E021 — review row kept). One of 8 valid roles (accepted_values source test).

search_index.name: dim_projects
search_index.description: Clean project dimension: one row per project_id from stg_projects (the dedup survivors). Soft-issue rows (null name P013, null/invalid budget P008/P032/P019) are retained since the id is valid. Negative budget is nullified here. Contracted; project_id is a DB-enforced primary key and the FK target for fct_timesheets.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project PK (DB-enforced primary key). One row per project_id (35 rows).

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Project name; nullable (NULL for P013 — review row kept).

search_index.name: budget
search_index.description: Project budget as decimal(12,2); NULL for P008 (non-numeric source), P019 (negative, nullified), P032 (null source). Review rows kept with NULL budget.

search_index.name: fct_timesheets
search_index.description: Clean timesheets fact mart: the accepted fact set from int_timesheets_validated left-joined to dim_employees and dim_projects. Pure join, no filters — int already removed dangling FKs and single-source rejects, so the grain is clean (one row per timesheet_id). timesheet_id is a DB-enforced primary key; employee_id / project_id FK integrity is enforced upstream by int_timesheets_validated (prevention) and asserted here by relationships tests (detection).

search_index.name: timesheet_id
search_index.description: Fact PK (DB-enforced primary key). Deterministic md5 surrogate of the natural key (employee_id|project_id|date|hours). One row per accepted timesheet (331 rows).

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Employee FK → dim_employees.employee_id. Guaranteed valid (dangling rows filtered in int_timesheets_validated). Asserted by relationships test.

search_index.name: employee_name
search_index.description: Denormalized employee name from dim_employees (for query convenience).

search_index.name: employee_role
search_index.description: Denormalized employee role from dim_employees (for query convenience).

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project FK → dim_projects.project_id. Guaranteed valid (dangling rows filtered in int_timesheets_validated). Asserted by relationships test.

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Denormalized project name from dim_projects (for query convenience).

search_index.name: project_budget
search_index.description: Denormalized project budget from dim_projects (for query convenience). NULL for review projects.

search_index.name: date
search_index.description: Timesheet date; non-null (bad dates removed in staging). Parsed from 3 source formats (ISO / dd/mm/yyyy / dd-Mon-yy).

search_index.name: hours
search_index.description: Hours worked; non-null, in range (0, 24] (non-numeric and out-of-range removed in staging).

search_index.name: int_timesheets_validated
search_index.description: The accepted fact set: stg_timesheets semi-joined to the clean stg_employees / stg_projects (IN subqueries, no fan-out) to filter dangling FKs. Feeds fct_timesheets. The only intermediate (connects multiple atomic sources).

search_index.name: timesheet_id
search_index.description: Deterministic surrogate PK (md5 of natural key) from stg_timesheets; one per accepted timesheet row.

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Employee FK; guaranteed non-dangling after the semi-join filter to stg_employees.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project FK; guaranteed non-dangling after the semi-join filter to stg_projects.

search_index.name: date
search_index.description: Parsed timesheet date (ISO / dd/mm/yyyy / dd-Mon-yy); non-null and valid (bad dates removed in staging).

search_index.name: hours
search_index.description: Hours worked; non-null, in range (0, 24] (non-numeric and out-of-range removed in staging).

search_index.name: rpt_project_economics
search_index.description: Per-project economics: budget + logged hours + derived budget_per_hour (budget / total_hours). LEFT-joins from dim_projects so ALL projects appear (budgeted-but-not-started show 0 hours). ASSUMPTION: budget_per_hour is meaningful only if budget is a labor-cost budget; otherwise indicative only.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project PK (DB-enforced). One row per project (35 rows, including projects with no timesheets).

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Project name from dim_projects.

search_index.name: budget
search_index.description: Project budget from dim_projects. NULL for review projects (P008/P019/P032).

search_index.name: total_hours
search_index.description: Total logged hours for this project (0 for budgeted-but-not-started projects).

search_index.name: distinct_employees
search_index.description: Count of distinct employees who logged hours (0 for projects with no timesheets).

search_index.name: budget_per_hour
search_index.description: Derived: budget / total_hours. NULL when total_hours = 0 or budget is NULL. Assumes budget is a labor-cost budget (see model description).

search_index.name: stg_employees
search_index.description: Typed, deduped projection of raw.employees: one row per non-null employee_id (dup_rank = 1 survivor). Soft-issue rows (null name/role) are KEPT since the id is valid.

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Clean employee identifier (non-null; exact duplicates removed).

search_index.name: name
search_index.description: Employee name (NULL where empty -- E013).

search_index.name: role
search_index.description: Employee role (NULL where empty -- E021).

search_index.name: stg_projects
search_index.description: Typed, deduped projection of raw.projects: one row per non-null project_id (dup_rank = 1 survivor). try_cast budget to decimal (non-numeric -> NULL). Soft-issue rows (null name, null/invalid/negative budget) are KEPT; negative budget is nullified in dim_projects.

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Clean project identifier (non-null; exact duplicates removed).

search_index.name: project_name
search_index.description: Project name (NULL where the source field was empty -- P013).

search_index.name: budget
search_index.description: Budget as decimal(12,2); non-numeric source values (e.g. '55000-60000' for P008) -> NULL. Negative (P019) preserved here, nullified in dim_projects.

search_index.name: stg_timesheets
search_index.description: Typed, deduped projection of raw.timesheets with single-source hard rejects removed (missing employee_id, unparseable date, non-numeric/out-of-range hours). Cross-source dangling FKs (E099/E050/P999) are KEPT here and removed in int_timesheets_validated. Adds a deterministic surrogate PK timesheet_id.

search_index.name: timesheet_id
search_index.description: Deterministic surrogate PK = md5(employee_id|project_id|date|hours).

search_index.name: employee_id
search_index.description: Employee identifier (non-null; single-source rejects with null employee_id removed; dangling E099/E050 retained).

search_index.name: project_id
search_index.description: Project identifier (non-null; dangling P999 retained).

search_index.name: date
search_index.description: Parsed date via coalesce(try_cast date, try_strptime %d/%m/%Y, try_strptime %d-%b-%y); unparseable rows removed.

search_index.name: hours
search_index.description: Hours as decimal(5,2); non-numeric rows removed, out-of-range (<=0 or >24) rows removed.
